rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settltment Agreement
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 2] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Stephen M. Murphy. As often happens in probate proceedings, several of the parties to this matter share a surname. To reduce confusion, the court refers to parties by their first names in this tentative ruling, with no disrespect intended. Before the court is Co-Conservators Lauro Trias and Ramona Figueira's Motion to Enforce Settlement Agreement, pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 664.6. On November 12, 2021, the court entered Order Granting Petition for Approval of Amended Settlement Agreement. The parties to the Settlement were four of Leonisa Trias' seven children, Lauro Trias, Ramona Figueira, Prestina Macias, Lazaro Trias, Jr., and Prestina's husband Carlos C. Macias. Relevant to this motion, the terms of the settlement addressed: an 850 and Disinheritance Petition the Co-Conservators had filed in the conservatorship; transferring title to 121 Seneca Avenue out of Prestina and Carlos' names and back to the conservatee, and plans to ultimately transfer title to the 2004 trust; liens and other potential claims Prestina and her husband had against the conservatorship estate; Prestina, Carlos, and Lazaro's residence at 121 Seneca Avenue during conservatee's lifetime; sale of 121 Seneca Avenue after conservatee's death. The motion seeks to enforce the settlement against Prestina Macias, who has allegedly not performed terms of the settlement, specifically refusing to sign deeds to return title on the real property located at 121 Seneca Avenue to the estate. No opposition to the motion was filed. Background: In August 2004, Leonisa executed a trust, but did not fund it. In 2015, she executed a Durable Power of Attorney, nominating her son Lauro as her agent. From 2016 to 2019, her care and finances were managed by her children Lauro Trias, Ramona Figueria, Prestina Macias. Prestina lived with her mother and was the primary caregiver. In or around early 2019, Leonisa executed a second Durable Power of Attorney, nominating Prestina as her agent. In or around early 2019, title to Leonia's residence was transferred from her name to Prestina and Carlos's names. On September 4, 2019, Lauro Trias and Ramona Figueria filed Petition for Conservator of Person and Estate of Leonisa Trias. The petition was granted on July 6, 2020, over opposition by Leonisa and Prestina through their respective attorneys. [Part 1 of 2]

rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settltment Agreement (Added For Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 2] Also on July 6, 2020, the court ordered the matter to pro bono mediation to address the parties' disputes over Prestina allegedly isolating Leonisa from her family and nominated agents, Leonisa's medical care, and the contested real property transfer and related financial activity by Prestina and Carlos of 121 Seneca Avenue. These mediation efforts and further court direction eventually yielded the Amended Settlement Agreement, which was approved by the court on November 12, 2021. On November 19, 2021, Co-Conservators dismissed their 850 and Disinheritance petition, pursuant to the Amended Settlement Agreement. On January 13, 2022, after hearing the court granted Co-Conservator's Ex Parte Application for Order Voiding of Prior Transfer Document, pursuant to the Amended Settlement Agreement. The order voided the document executed on April 17, 2019 transferring title to the real property located at 121 Seneca Avenue from Leonisa to Prestina and Carlos, and ruled the property reverted back to Leonisa as her separate property. The motion does not directly address what effect this order has had on transferring title back to the estate. Leonisa died on January 6, 2022, intestate. Despite the stated intention of the Amended Settlement Agreement, the real property had not been transferred On October 25, 2022, Lauro was appointed Administrator of the probate estate with limited authority under IAEA and $20,000 bond, in related case number PES-22-305443. There is a status hearing on close of estate currently set for October 30, 2023. The unopposed Motion to Enforce Settlement Agreement is GRANTED. Prestina Macias is ordered to execute all necessary documents to transfer title to the real property located at 121 Seneca Avenue, San Francisco, CA, to the estate of Leonisa Trias, pursuant to Section 2, sub. (d) of the Amended Settlement Agreement. Prestina Macias is ordered to execute these necessary documents within fourteen (14) days of service of Notice of Entry of Order. The Amended Settlement Agreement at Section 14 contains a fee-shifting agreement in the event of a successful motion to enforce settlement under Code of Civil Procedure section 664.6. However, the request for an award of fees and costs is not properly before the court on this motion. The Notice of Motion does not give notice of a request for fees and costs pursuant to the Amended Settlement Agreement. The motion requests an award of unspecified fees and costs for the first time and only in the conclusion to the Memorandum of Points and Authorities. Only the proposed order lodged with the motion lists a specific amount of requested fees and costs. And there is no evidentiary showing offered to support a finding that the amount requested in the lodged, unfiled proposed order is reasonable. Co-Conservators may seek an award of reasonable fees and costs pursuant to the Amended Settlement Agreement on a separately noticed motion. Co-Conservators shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. [Part 2 of 2]
